---
title: "About Me"
---

I am an MPH candidate in Global Health at Emory University’s Rollins School of Public Health, with interests in health equity, humanitarian emergencies, health systems, and applied public health data science.

My graduate work has included projects in data visualization, machine learning, public health research, and program planning. I am interested in using data and communication to make public health information more accessible, actionable, and useful for decision-making.

Outside of coursework, I have been involved in leadership, teaching support, and student-facing roles at Emory. These experiences have strengthened my interest in collaborative problem-solving and practical public health work.
